codeReview.summary: Pass. Both targeted scroll handlers now use the same requestAnimationFrame throttling pattern: each raw scroll event cancels any pending frame, schedules a single RAF callback to read window.scrollY and call setScrollY, and removes the listener plus cancels the pending RAF on cleanup. This satisfies VAL-RERENDER-003 and preserves the existing scrollY-driven header border behavior while avoiding direct state updates on every raw scroll event.

summary: Reviewed the rerender-throttle-scroll feature diff, handoff, transcript skeleton, and shared-state docs. The implementation correctly throttles both specified scroll handlers, cleans up safely, and introduces no reviewable regressions, so the feature passes.
